command: החלטת מיתוג: המוצר יקרא 'Wboty'. המשתמשים (בעלי העסקים) יראו את המותג Wboty בכל מקום בממשק. הטכנולוגיה הבסיסית נשארת ללא שינוי — Baileys על שרת Ubuntu. אין לשנות שום קוד עדיין — זה planning בלבד. מה צריך לעדכן מבחינת מיתוג (שמות, לוגו, טקסטים, domain) ומה הצעדים המעשיים?

plan.summary: Plan the rebranding from 'WhatsApp Agent' to 'Wboty' across the product interface, documentation, and domains without changing underlying code.

tasks.instruction: Update all user-facing text in the UI to replace 'WhatsApp Agent' with 'Wboty' brand name, including page titles, headers, buttons, tooltips, and error messages, ensuring Hebrew language support and RTL compatibility.
tasks.context: Current product name is 'WhatsApp Agent' used throughout the interface. Rebrand to 'Wboty' while keeping technical terms like 'WhatsApp' where accurate. No code changes yet—focus on text mapping.

tasks.instruction: Design a simple logo concept for 'Wboty' that fits the B2B SaaS aesthetic and can be implemented with CSS/SVG, considering RTL layout and color scheme consistency.
tasks.context: Logo should be minimal, scalable, and work in both light/dark modes. Provide SVG/CSS specs for frontend implementation later.

tasks.instruction: Identify domain and deployment changes needed for rebranding, including updating Vercel project name, environment variables (e.g., NEXT_PUBLIC_APP_NAME), and planning domain migration from current to 'wboty.com' or similar.
tasks.context: Current deployment uses Vercel with existing domain. Plan steps for domain purchase, DNS configuration, and environment updates without breaking live service.

tasks.instruction: Update documentation (README, internal docs) to reflect 'Wboty' brand name, including project description, setup instructions, and API references.
tasks.context: All documentation currently references 'WhatsApp Agent'. Update text only, no code changes.

tasks.instruction: Define acceptance criteria for rebranding rollout, including UI text verification, logo display, domain functionality, and regression testing to ensure no underlying code is affected.
tasks.context: Rebranding is cosmetic—ensure no changes to auth, database, WhatsApp functionality, or session-manager. Plan test cases for multi-tenant isolation and Hebrew/RTL support.

results.output:
# Wboty Rebranding - Text Mapping & UX Guidelines

## 1. Brand Name Application Rules

**Wboty** (pronounced "W-bot-ee") should be used as:
- The product name (replacing "WhatsApp Agent")
- The brand name in all marketing/UI contexts
- **Never** modify to "Wboty's" or "Wboties" - use possessive form in Hebrew when needed

**WhatsApp** should remain when:
- Referring to the actual WhatsApp platform/connection
- Technical contexts (WhatsApp Web, WhatsApp messages)
- Legal/disclaimer contexts

## 2. Complete Text Replacement Map

### Page Titles & Headers
| Current Text | New Text (English) | Hebrew Translation | Notes |
|-------------|-------------------|-------------------|-------|
| WhatsApp Agent Dashboard | Wboty Dashboard | לוח הבקרה של Wboty | |
| WhatsApp Agent Settings | Wboty Settings | הגדרות Wboty | |
| WhatsApp Agent Capabilities | Wboty Capabilities | יכולות Wboty | |
| WhatsApp Agent Chat | Wboty Chat | צ'אט Wboty | |
| WhatsApp Agent Contacts | Wboty Contacts | אנשי קשר של Wboty | |

### Tab Labels
| Current | New (EN) | Hebrew | RTL Note |
|---------|----------|--------|----------|
| Connect WhatsApp | Connect WhatsApp | התחברות לוואטסאפ | Keep "WhatsApp" |
| Settings | Settings | הגדרות | |
| Capabilities | Capabilities | יכולות | |
| Chat | Chat | צ'אט | |
| Contacts | Contacts | אנשי קשר | |

### Buttons & CTAs
| Current | New (EN) | Hebrew | Context |
|---------|----------|--------|---------|
| Create WhatsApp Agent | Create Wboty | צור Wboty | Onboarding |
| Save WhatsApp Agent Settings | Save Wboty Settings | שמור הגדרות Wboty | |
| Disconnect WhatsApp Agent | Disconnect Wboty | נתק את Wboty | Destructive action |
| WhatsApp Agent is Learning | Wboty is Learning | Wboty לומד | Status indicator |
| WhatsApp Agent is Active | Wboty is Active | Wboty פעיל | Status indicator |
| WhatsApp Agent is Paused | Wboty is Paused | Wboty מושהה | Status indicator |

### Status Messages & Tooltips
| Current | New (EN) | Hebrew | Display Context |
|---------|----------|--------|-----------------|
| WhatsApp Agent connected | Wboty connected | Wboty מחובר | Green status badge |
| WhatsApp Agent disconnected | Wboty disconnected | Wboty מנותק | Red status badge |
| WhatsApp Agent learning mode | Wboty learning mode | מצב למידה של Wboty | Tooltip |
| WhatsApp Agent will reply automatically | Wboty will reply automatically | Wboty ישיב אוטומטית | Tooltip |
| WhatsApp Agent is paused | Wboty is paused | Wboty מושהה | Tooltip |

### Error Messages
| Current | New (EN) | Hebrew | Severity |
|---------|----------|--------|----------|
| WhatsApp Agent failed to connect | Wboty failed to connect | Wboty נכשל בהתחברות | Error |
| WhatsApp Agent configuration error | Wboty configuration error | שגיאת הגדרה של Wboty | Error |
| WhatsApp Agent is not responding | Wboty is not responding | Wboty לא מגיב | Warning |
| WhatsApp Agent rate limit exceeded | Wboty rate limit exceeded | Wboty חרג ממגבלת השימוש | Warning |

### Empty States & Onboarding
| Current | New (EN) | Hebrew | Component |
|---------|----------|--------|-----------|
| No WhatsApp Agent configured | No Wboty configured | אין Wboty מוגדר | Empty state |
| Set up your WhatsApp Agent | Set up your Wboty | הגדר את ה-Wboty שלך | CTA button |
| Your WhatsApp Agent will appear here | Your Wboty will appear here | ה-Wboty שלך יופיע כאן | Placeholder |
| Welcome to WhatsApp Agent! | Welcome to Wboty! | ברוכים הבאים ל-Wboty! | Onboarding headline |

### Knowledge Base Contexts
| Current | New (EN) | Hebrew | Usage |
|---------|----------|--------|-------|
| WhatsApp Agent Knowledge Base | Wboty Knowledge Base | בסיס הידע של Wboty | Section header |
| Teach your WhatsApp Agent | Teach your Wboty | למד את ה-Wboty שלך | Instructional text |
| What your WhatsApp Agent knows | What your Wboty knows | מה שה-Wboty שלך יודע | Info panel |

## 3. RTL Layout Considerations

### Hebrew Text Guidelines:
1. **Wboty stays Latin**: Always display "Wboty" in Latin characters, even in Hebrew sentences
2. **Spacing**: Ensure proper spacing around Latin brand name in Hebrew text
3. **Directionality**: Mixed LTR/RTL text should flow correctly:
   - Hebrew sentence + "Wboty" = RTL with LTR brand
   - Example: `לוח הבקרה של Wboty` (RTL flow, Wboty as LTR island)

### Visual Hierarchy:
- **Primary headers**: Hebrew (RTL) with Wboty in Latin
- **Secondary labels**: Match language of parent section
- **Technical terms**: Keep English if no common Hebrew equivalent exists

## 4. Implementation Priority Order

### Phase 1: Critical User-Facing Text (High Impact)
1. Page titles & main headers
2. Tab labels (except "Connect WhatsApp")
3. Status badges & indicators
4. Primary CTAs (Create, Save, Disconnect)
5. Empty states & onboarding

### Phase 2: Secondary UI Text (Medium Impact)
1. Tooltips & hover states
2. Error/warning messages
3. Success confirmations
4. Instructional text
5. Modal/dialog titles

### Phase 3: Technical & Legal Contexts (Low Impact)
1. API error messages (backend-originated)
2. Legal disclaimers
3. Documentation references
4. Console/log messages

## 5. Special Cases & Edge Conditions

### Mixed Contexts:
- **"Connect WhatsApp to Wboty"** (not "Connect WhatsApp to WhatsApp Agent")
- **"Wboty uses WhatsApp Web"** (technical accuracy maintained)
- **"Your Wboty WhatsApp connection"** (clear relationship)

### Possessive Forms:
- English: "Your Wboty's settings" → "Your Wboty settings" (avoid apostrophe)
- Hebrew: Use construct state: `הגדרות ה-Wboty שלך`

### Plural References:
- Never use "Wbotys" or "Wboties"
- Instead: "Your Wboty instances" or "Multiple Wboty accounts"

## 6. Accessibility Notes

1. **Screen Readers**: Ensure "Wboty" is pronounced correctly (spell out: W-B-O-T-Y)
2. **Focus Indicators**: No change needed from existing patterns
3. **Color Contrast**: Brand green (#25D366) remains primary action color
4. **ARIA Labels**: Update to include new brand name where relevant

## 7. Success Metrics

- ✅ All user-facing text uses "Wboty" instead of "WhatsApp Agent"
- ✅ WhatsApp remains in technical/connection contexts
- ✅ Hebrew translations maintain proper RTL flow
- ✅ No broken layout from mixed-direction text
- ✅ Consistent brand application across all UI components

---

**Next Step**: Provide this mapping to the Frontend Developer for implementation. Recommend starting with `src/components/tenant/` components and working outward to page titles and global UI elements.

# Domain & Deployment Rebranding Plan

## Current State Analysis
- **Vercel project**: Current name unknown (need to check Vercel dashboard)
- **Existing domain**: Unknown (need to check current DNS)
- **Target domain**: `wboty.com` (or similar - confirm exact domain)
- **Live service**: WhatsApp sessions must remain uninterrupted

## Phase 1: Preparation (Before DNS Changes)

### 1.1 Environment Variables Update
Add to `.env.local.example`:
```env
# Branding
NEXT_PUBLIC_APP_NAME="WhatsApp Agent"
NEXT_PUBLIC_APP_DOMAIN="wboty.com"
NEXT_PUBLIC_BRAND_COLOR="#2563eb" # Example blue
```

### 1.2 Code Updates
Update `src/app/layout.tsx` to use new branding:
```tsx
// Add to metadata
export const metadata = {
  title: process.env.NEXT_PUBLIC_APP_NAME || 'WhatsApp Agent',
  // ... other metadata
}
```

Update any hardcoded brand references in:
- `src/components/tenant/*` components
- Email templates (if any)
- Terms/Privacy pages

### 1.3 Vercel Project Rename
```bash
# Check current project
vercel projects

# Rename project (if needed)
vercel project rename <current-name> <new-name>
```
**Note**: Project rename doesn't affect URLs unless custom domain is changed.

## Phase 2: Domain Acquisition & DNS Setup

### 2.1 Domain Purchase
1. Purchase `wboty.com` (or chosen domain) from registrar (Namecheap, GoDaddy, etc.)
2. Ensure domain supports:
   - A records (for root domain)
   - CNAME records (for www subdomain)
   - TXT records (for email verification if needed)

### 2.2 DNS Pre-configuration
At registrar's DNS settings, prepare but **DON'T ACTIVATE**:
```
Type    Name    Value                    TTL
A       @       76.76.21.21              Auto  # Vercel IP
CNAME   www     cname.vercel-dns.com.    Auto  # Vercel CNAME
```

## Phase 3: Parallel Deployment Strategy

### 3.1 New Vercel Project (Optional but Recommended)
Create new Vercel project for rebranded version:
```bash
# Clone current repo to new directory
git clone <repo-url> whatsapp-agent-rebranded
cd whatsapp-agent-rebranded

# Deploy to new Vercel project
vercel --prod --name whatsapp-agent-rebranded
```

**Advantages**:
- Zero downtime during transition
- Can test new branding fully
- Rollback is instant (just switch DNS back)

### 3.2 Environment Configuration
In new Vercel project dashboard:
1. Copy all existing environment variables
2. Add new branding variables:
   - `NEXT_PUBLIC_APP_NAME`
   - `NEXT_PUBLIC_APP_DOMAIN`
3. Update any domain-specific variables (like callback URLs if using OAuth)

## Phase 4: DNS Migration (Cutover)

### 4.1 Pre-cutover Checklist
✅ New Vercel project deployed and tested  
✅ Session-manager updated with new domain (if callback URLs changed)  
✅ All environment variables verified  
✅ Branding changes visible in new deployment  
✅ Old deployment still accessible via old domain  

### 4.2 DNS Change Procedure
1. **Set low TTL** (300 seconds) on old domain 24h before cutover
2. **Add new domain to Vercel**:
   ```bash
   vercel domains add wboty.com
   vercel domains add www.wboty.com
   ```
3. **Update DNS records** at registrar:
   - Activate pre-configured A and CNAME records
   - Keep old domain records active during transition
4. **Verify propagation**:
   ```bash
   dig wboty.com
   dig www.wboty.com
   ```

### 4.3 Post-cutover Verification
1. Check `https://wboty.com` loads correctly
2. Verify API endpoints work
3. Test WhatsApp webhook delivery (critical!)
4. Monitor session-manager logs for any domain-related issues

## Phase 5: Session Manager Updates

### 5.1 Environment Variables
Update `session-manager/.env.local`:
```env
# Update if using domain in any callbacks
APP_BASE_URL=https://wboty.com
```

### 5.2 Webhook Configuration
If WhatsApp webhooks use domain in URLs:
1. Update Supabase webhook configuration (if stored)
2. Restart session-manager:
   ```bash
   cd session-manager
   pm2 restart session-mgr
   ```

## Phase 6: Cleanup & Monitoring

### 6.1 Old Domain Decommissioning
After 48h of stable operation:
1. Remove old domain from Vercel
2. Update DNS TTL back to normal (86400)
3. Archive old Vercel project (keep for rollback)

### 6.2 Monitoring Plan
Watch for:
- **Session drops**: Monitor session-manager logs for reconnection spikes
- **Webhook failures**: Check Supabase logs for failed deliveries
- **User reports**: Monitor support channels for login/issues

## Rollback Procedure
If issues occur within 24h:
1. **Immediate**: Revert DNS to old domain settings
2. **Fallback**: Switch Vercel production alias back to old domain
3. **Session recovery**: Session-manager should reconnect automatically
4. **Investigate**: Check logs for root cause before retrying

## Critical Success Factors
1. **WhatsApp sessions persist** - Baileys auth state stored in Supabase, independent of domain
2. **Webhook continuity** - Ensure `POST /api/webhook` endpoint remains accessible
3. **Database connections** - Supabase URLs remain unchanged
4. **User authentication** - Supabase Auth redirect URLs updated if changed

## Estimated Timeline
- **Day 1-2**: Code updates, new Vercel project setup
- **Day 3**: DNS pre-configuration, testing
- **Day 4**: Cutover (off-hours recommended)
- **Day 5-6**: Monitoring, cleanup

## Required Actions from You
1. **Confirm current domain** (to plan DNS changes)
2. **Confirm exact new domain** (wboty.com or other)
3. **Check Vercel project name** (to decide if rename needed)
4. **Identify any hardcoded domain references** in codebase

**Next step**: Share current domain details so I can provide specific DNS migration instructions.

# Wboty Documentation Update Plan

## Files to Update

### 1. **README.md** (root level)
```markdown
# Wboty - WhatsApp Automation Platform

Wboty is a multi-tenant B2B SaaS platform that enables businesses to automate their WhatsApp customer support using AI.

## Key Features
- 🤖 AI-powered WhatsApp responses using DeepSeek
- 🏢 Multi-tenant architecture for business clients
- 📚 Continuous learning from customer interactions
- ⚙️ Granular contact filtering (whitelist/blacklist)
- 📊 Real-time conversation dashboard

## Tech Stack
- **Frontend**: Next.js 16 (App Router), React 19, TypeScript
- **Backend**: Next.js API Routes + Express session manager
- **Database**: Supabase (PostgreSQL)
- **WhatsApp**: Baileys WebSocket library
- **AI**: DeepSeek API (OpenAI-compatible)

## Quick Start
1. Clone repository
2. Set up environment variables
3. Deploy to Vercel + session manager server
4. Configure your first tenant

[See full setup instructions →](#setup)
```

### 2. **session-manager/README.md**
```markdown
# Wboty Session Manager

The always-on service that manages WhatsApp WebSocket connections for Wboty tenants.

## Responsibilities
- Maintain persistent WhatsApp WebSocket connections via Baileys
- Process incoming WhatsApp messages
- Generate AI responses using DeepSeek
- Run batch learning jobs
- Store session state in Supabase

## Deployment
This service must run 24/7 on a dedicated server/VPS. It cannot run on serverless platforms.
```

### 3. **docs/API.md** (if exists)
- Update all references from "WhatsApp Agent API" to "Wboty API"
- Update endpoint descriptions to mention Wboty platform

### 4. **docs/SETUP.md** (if exists)
- Update title: "Wboty Setup Guide"
- Update introduction to reflect Wboty branding
- Keep all technical instructions unchanged

### 5. **package.json** (root and session-manager)
```json
{
  "name": "wboty",
  "description": "Wboty - WhatsApp Automation Platform for Businesses",
  "repository": {
    "url": "https://github.com/your-org/wboty"
  }
}
```

## Text Replacement Guidelines

Replace these phrases:
- "WhatsApp Agent" → "Wboty"
- "WhatsApp Agent platform" → "Wboty platform"
- "WhatsApp Agent SaaS" → "Wboty SaaS"
- "Agent" (when referring to the platform) → "Wboty"

**Do NOT replace:**
- "agent" when referring to AI agent functionality
- "agent_mode", "agent_prompt" (database fields)
- Code references, variable names, or environment variables
- Technical terms like "session manager", "AI agent"

## Implementation Steps

1. **Check for existing documentation files:**
```bash
find . -name "*.md" -type f | grep -v node_modules | grep -v .git
```

2. **Update README.md:**
```bash
# Backup original
cp README.md README.md.backup

# Use sed for bulk replacements (macOS/BSD syntax)
sed -i '' 's/WhatsApp Agent/Wboty/g' README.md
sed -i '' 's/WhatsApp Agent platform/Wboty platform/g' README.md
sed -i '' 's/WhatsApp Agent SaaS/Wboty SaaS/g' README.md

# Review changes
git diff README.md
```

3. **Update session-manager README:**
```bash
cd session-manager
sed -i '' 's/WhatsApp Agent/Wboty/g' README.md
cd ..
```

4. **Check for other documentation:**
```bash
# Look for markdown files in docs/ directory
ls -la docs/ 2>/dev/null || echo "No docs directory found"

# Look for any other documentation
find . -name "*.md" -exec grep -l "WhatsApp Agent" {} \;
```

5. **Update package.json descriptions:**
```bash
# Root package.json
sed -i '' 's/"WhatsApp Agent"/"Wboty"/g' package.json

# Session manager package.json
sed -i '' 's/"WhatsApp Agent"/"Wboty"/g' session-manager/package.json
```

## Verification Checklist

After updates:
- [ ] README.md reflects Wboty branding
- [ ] session-manager/README.md updated
- [ ] No code changes made (only documentation)
- [ ] Technical instructions remain unchanged
- [ ] Database schema references unchanged
- [ ] Environment variable names unchanged
- [ ] API endpoint paths unchanged

## Notes
- This is a documentation-only change
- No environment variables need updating
- No deployment required
- No database migrations needed
- The change is purely cosmetic for documentation

**Time estimate:** 15-30 minutes depending on number of documentation files

synthesis.implementation_plan:
1. **Phase 1: Code & Documentation Updates (No Deployment)**
   - **Update Frontend Text:** Replace all instances of 'WhatsApp Agent' with 'Wboty' in UI components. Start with `src/components/tenant/` as per UX mapping. Use the provided text replacement map for English and Hebrew.
   - **Update Documentation:** Run `sed` commands to replace 'WhatsApp Agent' with 'Wboty' in all `.md` files (e.g., `README.md`, `session-manager/README.md`). Do not change code references, variable names, or environment variables.
   - **Update Package Metadata:** Modify `description` fields in `package.json` (root and `session-manager/`) to reflect Wboty branding.
   - **Implement Logo:** Create `src/components/Logo/WbotyLogo.tsx` and corresponding CSS module with SVG from UX specs. Update favicon in `public/` with simplified version.
   - **Update Layout Metadata:** In `src/app/layout.tsx`, update `metadata.title` to use a new environment variable (e.g., `NEXT_PUBLIC_APP_NAME`) set to 'Wboty'.

2. **Phase 2: Environment & Domain Preparation**
   - **Acquire Domain:** Purchase `wboty.com` (or confirmed domain) from a registrar.
   - **Configure Environment Variables:** Add `NEXT_PUBLIC_APP_NAME='Wboty'` and `NEXT_PUBLIC_APP_DOMAIN='wboty.com'` to `.env.local.example` and Vercel project settings.
   - **Prepare DNS Records:** At registrar, pre-configure (but do not activate) A record for `@` to Vercel IP (76.76.21.21) and CNAME for `www` to `cname.vercel-dns.com.`.
   - **Optional New Vercel Project:** Clone repo and deploy to a new Vercel project (e.g., 'whatsapp-agent-rebranded') for zero-downtime testing.

3. **Phase 3: Deployment & DNS Cutover**
   - **Deploy Updated Code:** Push changes and deploy to production Vercel project (or new project).
   - **Add Domain to Vercel:** Run `vercel domains add wboty.com` and `vercel domains add www.wboty.com`.
   - **Activate DNS:** Update registrar DNS to activate pre-configured A and CNAME records. Set TTL to 300 seconds 24h prior.
   - **Update Session Manager:** If webhook URLs use domain, update `APP_BASE_URL` in `session-manager/.env.local` and restart service (`pm2 restart session-mgr`).
   - **Verify Propagation:** Check `dig wboty.com` and test all routes (e.g., `/login`, `/api/*`).

4. **Phase 4: Testing & Verification**
   - **UI Testing:** Verify all text replacements using QA test suites (e.g., Playwright for brand text, logo display, RTL Hebrew).
   - **Functional Regression:** Test WhatsApp messaging, AI replies, multi-tenant isolation, and auth flows per QA criteria.
   - **Domain Testing:** Ensure new domain resolves, SSL works, and old bookmarks redirect.
   - **Monitor:** Watch session-manager logs for webhook failures and user reports for 48h.

5. **Phase 5: Cleanup**
   - **Remove Old Domain:** After 48h of stability, remove old domain from Vercel and revert DNS TTL to 86400.
   - **Archive Old Project:** If new Vercel project was created, archive the old one.
blockers: Confirm exact new domain (e.g., wboty.com) for purchase and DNS setup.
Identify current domain and Vercel project name to plan DNS migration.
Check for any hardcoded domain references in codebase (e.g., in API callbacks or email templates).
synthesis.pm_notes: Coordinate with team: UX for final logo approval, DevOps for DNS cutover timing (off-hours recommended), and QA for regression testing. Critical risk: Ensure WhatsApp sessions persist during domain change—Baileys auth is stored in Supabase, independent of domain. Rollback plan: If issues occur within 24h, revert DNS to old domain and switch Vercel alias back. No database migrations or code changes to core functionality (Baileys/DeepSeek) are needed.

feedbacks.what_worked: Comprehensive text mapping with clear English/Hebrew translations, proper RTL considerations, and phased implementation priorities. Excellent attention to brand name usage rules (e.g., avoiding possessive forms) and maintaining WhatsApp references in technical contexts.
feedbacks.what_to_improve: Could include a quick audit script to find all 'WhatsApp Agent' strings in the codebase for the developer. Also, consider adding a note about updating any hardcoded brand references in email templates or external integrations.
feedbacks.memory_update: For rebranding tasks, always provide both English and Hebrew translations with RTL layout guidance, and explicitly define where the old brand (WhatsApp) should remain vs. be replaced.

feedbacks.what_worked: Logo design is well-conceived with clear SVG specs, React component implementation, and RTL/accessibility considerations. Good use of CSS variables for theming and providing favicon variants.
feedbacks.what_to_improve: Suggest adding a monochrome version for printing or low-color contexts. Also, include a note about testing the logo in various background colors beyond just dark/light modes.
feedbacks.memory_update: When designing logos for rebranding, always provide SVG implementation with CSS variables for easy theming, and include favicon and app icon variants.

feedbacks.what_worked: Solid domain migration plan with parallel deployment strategy, DNS pre-configuration, and rollback procedure. Good emphasis on WhatsApp session persistence and webhook continuity.
feedbacks.what_to_improve: The plan assumes Vercel usage but doesn't confirm the current setup. Should explicitly ask for current domain and Vercel project details upfront. Also, the environment variable example uses a blue color (#2563eb) instead of the WhatsApp green (#25D366) mentioned in the logo design.
feedbacks.memory_update: For domain rebranding tasks, always request current domain and hosting details first, and ensure color variables align with the brand guidelines provided by UX.

feedbacks.what_worked: Clear documentation update plan with specific file targets and text replacement guidelines. Good distinction between what to change (brand names) and what not to change (code references, env vars).
feedbacks.what_to_improve: The sed commands use macOS syntax; should note Linux/Windows alternatives or recommend a cross-platform tool. Also, could include a step to update any OpenGraph or meta tags in the HTML for social sharing.
feedbacks.memory_update: When updating documentation for rebranding, provide cross-platform commands for text replacement and include meta tag updates for social media consistency.

feedbacks.what_worked: Thorough acceptance criteria and test plans covering UI, logo, domain functionality, and critical regression areas. Excellent inclusion of multi-tenant isolation tests and Hebrew/RTL support validation.
feedbacks.what_to_improve: The test code examples are extensive but could be summarized more concisely. Also, suggest adding a smoke test checklist for post-deployment quick verification.
feedbacks.memory_update: For rebranding QA, always include multi-tenant isolation tests and RTL language support checks, as these are critical for maintaining core functionality.
